channel.title: The Hungarian Daily
channel.description: Your essential daily English-language briefing on Hungarian politics, business, and culture.
channel.itunes:author: Eric Chi

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-14
item.description: Good morning, afternoon, or evening, depending on where youre tuning in from! And a very warm welcome to The Hungarian Daily, your essential podcast for foreign professionals, expats, digital nomads, and international business executives navigating life and work here in Budapest, Hungary.It is Monday, April 13, 2026, and what a seismic shift we have to discuss today. If youre just waking up, or perhaps still reeling from the weekends events, buckle up. Hungary has just witnessed a political earthquake of historic proportions, and the tremors are already being felt across the economy and beyond.Our top story, dominating every headline and conversation, is the **landslide victory of Péter Magyar** (Pay-ter Mah-djar) and his Tisza (Tih-sah) Party in yesterday’s parliamentary elections. This outcome has swept away the Orbán (Or-bahn) era, bringing to an end Viktor Orbáns remarkable 16-year tenure as Prime Minister. Its a moment that many thought might never come, and the capital, Budapest (Boo-da-pesht), erupted into spontaneous celebrations last night, with Reuters reporting that the city truly turned into a party zone as the news broke.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-15
item.description: Jó reggelt, Budapest! And a very warm welcome to The Hungarian Daily, your essential guide to news and insights for foreign professionals, expats, digital nomads, and international business executives living and working right here in Hungary. Im your host, and Im thrilled to have you join us this morning, Wednesday, April 15, 2026.Weve got a packed agenda today, diving into everything from the evolving political landscape to key economic indicators and cultural happenings that shape your life in this vibrant country. While all our top stories today carry a similar urgency score, rest assured, weve meticulously curated them to bring you the most relevant and impactful information, with a special emphasis on how these developments resonate with our international community.Lets kick things off with a look at the economic pulse of Hungary, as this directly impacts your investments, your cost of living, and the overall business environment.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-18
item.description: An EU delegation is set to meet Hungarys incoming government amid Polish hopes for a policy shift on Ukraine aid, while the Forint strengthens to a multi-year high post-election.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-19
item.description: Todays Hungarian Daily highlights Péter Magyars high-level talks with Brussels to unblock billions in EU funds, signaling a potential economic and political turning point for Hungary, with a new government aiming to fix the economy. We also cover Budapest Airports new terminal development, the future of the Visegrad Four, and a brief note on Russian Foreign Minister Lavrovs comments, alongside our essential Currency Corner and a look at quirky social media trends.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-20
item.description: Today, Monday, April 20, 2026, The Hungarian Daily focuses on the new Tisza governments potential to unlock billions in frozen EU funds, a critical development for Hungarys economy and international standing, alongside a strengthening Forint and rising real wages. We also discuss energy security, Norways support for democratic reforms, and local expat queries for planning May 1st activities and bachelor parties in Budapest.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-21
item.description: Todays Hungarian Daily reports on the formation of the new Tisza government, with Prime Minister-elect Péter Magyar announcing the first seven ministers and a sixteen-ministry cabinet, sparking discussions about economic reform and EU relations. We also cover positive news on net earnings outpacing inflation, the evolving dynamics of the Visegrad Four alliance, and Greenpeaces findings of no toxic pollution around Hungarys battery plants, before diving into expat-relevant social media topics on Budapest parking rules and finding specific imported goods.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-23
item.description: Today on The Hungarian Daily, we explore a stunning new poll indicating a potential decline for Orbáns Fidesz party and the rise of Péter Magyars Tisza party, signaling a major political realignment in Hungary. We also cover the formation of the new government, key cabinet appointments, the future of education and Erasmus, and the Forints stability amidst global pressures, alongside positive shifts for Korean battery firms in Hungary.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-25
item.description: Todays top news and updates from Hungary for expats.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-26
item.description: Todays Hungarian Daily covers the critical economic challenges facing Hungarys new government, the positive business shifts for Korean battery firms, and the significant political rally in Budapest against Orbán ahead of the vote. We also discuss tax plans, economic confidence, Orbáns potential parliamentary exit, and the future of the V4 group, along with essential currency and weather briefings for expats.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-27
item.description: Today, Monday, April 27, 2026, The Hungarian Daily covered the EUs proposed industrial investment rules drawing Chinese criticism, Hungarys new economy minister Kapitány promising budget transparency amidst economic challenges, and a massive rally in Budapest urging the vote against Prime Minister Orbán. We also discussed U.S. Senator JD Vances support for Orbán, the potential future of the V4 group and EU decision-making without Orbáns veto, and updates on the Budapest-Belgrade railway, domestic political appointments, and Mayor Karácsonys urban renewal vision, alongside expat social media trends.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-28
item.description: Todays The Hungarian Daily covers Hungarys pivotal moment as the new government prepares for high-stakes discussions in Brussels on Wednesday to unlock crucial EU funds, with Budapest anticipating 700 billion Forints for urban development. The broadcast also delves into the post-election political landscape, including the Tisza Partys stance and former PM Orbáns decision not to take his mandate, while examining the challenges and opportunities for Hungarys economy and its relationship with the EU. Additionally, it touches on Tiszas ambitious healthcare reform plans, the impending launch of the Belgrade-Budapest passenger rail, and a look at current social media trends among expats.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-29
item.description: Todays broadcast covers a potential thaw in Hungary-EU relations, how Budapest companies are embracing workplace flexibility, and Wizz Airs plans for a major summer expansion.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-04-30
item.description: Todays Hungarian Daily covers the EUs call for credit of trust for the new Hungarian government, mixed Q1 GDP growth tempered by falling exports, and the business elites pivot away from Orbán. We also delve into the massive pre-election rally in Budapest, Senator Vances visit to support Orbán, and EU perspectives on Hungarys media freedom and veto power, all ahead of Sundays vote. Plus, weve got your Forint exchange rates, Budapests weather, and a look at social media trends.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-05-01
item.description: Todays Hungarian Daily covers a heated debate on alleged EU election interference, a significant drop in household financial savings, and the formation of a new Hungarian government under Magyar Péters Tisza party, alongside a pivot of the business elite away from Viktor Orbán, and international political endorsements. The show also includes a stable Forint exchange rate, an overcast but mild Budapest weather forecast, and a look at expat social media trends.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-05-02
item.description: Todays The Hungarian Daily focuses on Hungarys critical pre-election landscape, with a massive rally for change and intense scrutiny on the economy, including a reported pivot by the business elite away from Viktor Or-bahn. We also cover Hungarys EU relations, a newly disclosed Chinese loan, and positive Q1 GDP growth, alongside todays stable Forint exchange rates and a pleasant Budapest weather forecast.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-05-03
item.description: Ray hosts The Hungarian Daily on Sunday, May 03, 2026, covering Budapests clear skies and stable Forint. Top news includes expert predictions of a Forint strengthening ahead of potential euro adoption, warnings of significant EU funding losses due to tender cancellations, and calls from economic leaders to de-NER-ify the Hungarian economy. The show also discusses Péter Magyars political role, Trumps past vow to boost Hungarys economy, and a review of multinational subsidies, concluding with Budapest events and social media trends on rental cars.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-05-04
item.description: Todays Hungarian Daily podcast, hosted by Ray, delved into warnings of significant EU funding losses, clarified Prime Minister Orbáns political status amidst new opposition challenges, and analyzed economic shifts including deteriorating trade balances and US tariffs on EU exports. The episode also covered Tisza Partys reform plans, AIs job market impact, and Budapests vibrant cultural events.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-05-05
item.description: Ray hosts The Hungarian Daily on Tuesday, May 05, 2026, covering Budapests overcast weather (27.4°C/81.3°F high) and stable Forint exchange rates (1 EUR = 363.33 HUF, 1 USD = 310.54 HUF). Key news includes foreign capital returning to Hungarys property market amidst soaring Budapest prices, the IMFs warning of global inflation and meager growth due to Middle East conflict, and FocusEconomics analysis that Tiszas election win should support the economy despite fiscal constraints. The broadcast also discusses Tusks views on the Visegrad Group post-Orbán, disruptions in Hungarian large-value bank transfers, a Finra probe into Morgan Stanleys Hungary operation, and the National Banks abuse of power report. The show concludes with a look at Budapest events, a social media trend, and a call to action for listeners.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-05-06
item.description: Today, we delve into Hungarys ambitious, yet challenging, goal of adopting the euro by 2030, alongside a significant industrial boom led by battery production and the impact of a U.S. probe on Morgan Stanleys Budapest operations, all while exploring the evolving political landscape and practical expat insights.

item.title: The Hungarian Daily — 2026-05-07
item.description: Todays Hungarian Daily explores the governments ambitious plan for euro adoption by 2030 and significant Forint volatility, alongside the impact of windfall taxes on banks and a surge in retail, especially fuel, consumption. We also cover Budapests weather, upcoming events, and a quirky social media sidequest challenge.
